facts.text: Union City’s roots trace back to the Munsee-speaking Lenape, who first inhabited the area before Henry Hudson’s 1609 voyage.
The city’s early economy boomed with Swiss and Austrian immigrants who founded the European‑style lace‑making industry that made Union City famous.
Cuban immigrants began arriving in the late 1940s, drawn by abundant jobs in local embroidery and textile factories.
Many factory workers in Union City were Cuban‑American professionals who later shaped the city’s social and political landscape.
The Park Performing Arts Center, built in 1931 by a German congregation, still houses a 1,400‑seat theater and hosts local arts events.
In 2009, poet‑musician Graciela Barreto became Union City’s first poet laureate, highlighting the city’s vibrant cultural scene.
